purpose: Automatically inspect pull-request evidence, promote eligible drafts, and merge eligible exact heads without routing routine lifecycle work through the operator.
requiredGateDefinition: Checks from requiredWorkflowNames must be present and successful. Checks from conditionalRequiredWorkflowNames block when present but their absence does not deadlock unrelated PRs. Unlisted external checks and advisory platform/game workflows are reported but do not block readiness or automatic merge.

alwaysAdvisoryByDefault: Windows PowerShell 5.1 compatibility runs that require a Windows runner
advisoryValidation.reason: The product and development harness must remain modular across operating systems. A game-backed run on one installed system provides useful evidence but must not become the universal PR readiness or merge gate.
requiredInstead: cross-platform builds where the project supports them
advisoryValidation.workflowRule: Keep installed-game and OS-specific jobs in separately named advisory workflows. Do not place them in an always-required workflow whose completion controls readiness or merge. Path-scoped platform-neutral workflows belong in conditionalRequiredWorkflowNames.

draftControl.meaning: An explicitly labeled PR remains draft even after all required workflows pass. Without this label, eligible draft PRs are promoted automatically.

blockers: pull request is closed or already merged
pull request is still draft or carries a lifecycle hold label
required or present conditional check is pending, cancelled, or failed
head SHA changed after evidence inspection
GitHub reports the head as conflicting or mergeability is unknown
branch is behind when repository rules require synchronization
required review is missing or changes are requested
an unresolved review thread exists or review-thread inspection is incomplete
pull request predates the policy without the legacy opt-in label
pull request targets a non-default base without the stacked opt-in label
pull request comes from another repository without the fork opt-in label
GitHub repository rules reject both auto-merge and direct exact-head merge
mergeControl.githubFinalAuthority: The controller first requests GitHub auto-merge for the inspected exact head. If repository auto-merge is unavailable and the current merge state is clean, it attempts a direct exact-head squash merge. GitHub branch protection, rulesets, merge queues, and server-side head matching remain authoritative.

allowedActions: query checks, workflow conclusions, reviews, and review threads
classify always-required, conditional-required, and advisory validation
write a lifecycle result artifact and English workflow summary
mark an eligible draft pull request ready for review
request GitHub auto-merge for an eligible exact head
perform a direct exact-head squash merge only when the deterministic gate passes and GitHub accepts it
leave an already-ready or already-merged pull request unchanged
forbiddenActions: close a pull request without a separate closure disposition
merge a different head than the inspected head
commit or push unrelated repository changes
run untrusted pull-request code with a write-capable token
claim runtime or gameplay proof from lifecycle automation
authorizationRule: A repo-owned deterministic gate may authorize exact-head squash merge. Separate operator permission is not required after the gate passes. Closing, branch deletion, force-push, history rewrite, and unrelated commits remain outside this controller.

reachedByAutomation: exact-head automatic merge request and resulting GitHub state
notReachedByAutomation: build proof unless a required build workflow passed
